definition: A transient, non-blocking, in-page message that appears, is accessibly announced, persists for a bounded time (or until dismissed), and is removed. The general concept — temporary, dismissable, queued, severity-weighted, announced — of which a toast or snackbar is one presentation. Distinct from the OS-level Web Notifications API (system notifications outside the tab), from a persistent background-task surface, and from an inline (in-flow) message banner.
usage: Realized by the Notification block, which implements the Feedback Intent. Presentation styles (toast, snackbar, banner-position) belong to the implementation, not the standard.
